div.h2: HEAD vs GET Response Data Flow

p.em: Same resource · identical headers · body bytes differ

div.p: st.st_size → Content-Length st.st_mtime → Last-Modified Computed once; shared by both methods

Why Content-Length must be identical for GET and HEAD: RFC 7231 §4.3.2 — HEAD response headers MUST be identical to what GET would return. Caching proxies and CDNs use HEAD to validate cache freshness; a different Content-Length would corrupt cache state. Both methods derive Content-Length from st.st_size (fstat result) — never from counting bytes sent.

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
 Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
 Content-Length: 4096
 Last-Modified: Mon, 01 Jan 2024 12:00:00 GMT
 Connection: close

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
 Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
 Content-Length: 4096
 Last-Modified: Mon, 01 Jan 2024 12:00:00 GMT
 Connection: close
 
 ← no body bytes follow
